recent.statement: The US Federal Reserve will raise interest rates at the next FOMC meeting on July 28th.
recent.evidence: S7 shows the FOMC meeting is still scheduled for July 28, 8 days after the window closed, so no rate decision occurred within the forecast window.

recent.statement: A magnitude 5.8 earthquake will strike Mexico within the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: S1 shows a 5.5M earthquake in Mexico, below the forecast's 5.8 magnitude threshold, and no signal shows a 5.8+ event.

recent.statement: The US Federal Reserve will raise interest rates at their next meeting on July 28th
recent.evidence: The FOMC meeting is scheduled for July 28, still 8 days out and after the window closed, so no signal shows any rate increase occurred.

recent.statement: A Category 1 tropical cyclone will make landfall in the eastern United States within the next 24 hours.
recent.evidence: TWO-26 near the US East Coast was only a Tropical Depression (93 km/h), below Category 1 strength and no landfall of a Category 1 cyclone is shown, while FAUSTO-26 was in the East Pacific.

recent.statement: A tropical cyclone will make landfall in Mexico within the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: Neither signal shows a tropical cyclone making landfall in Mexico; TWO-26 was in the Atlantic near the US East Coast and FAUSTO-26 was an active East Pacific storm with no stated Mexican landfall.

recent.statement: A tropical cyclone will make landfall in Mexico within the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: Neither TWO-26 (Atlantic, off US east coast) nor FAUSTO-26 (East Pacific tropical storm) shows any landfall in Mexico within the window.

recent.statement: A magnitude 5.0 earthquake will occur in the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: S1 reports a magnitude 5.5 earthquake in Mexico on 2026-07-19 within the forecast window, exceeding the 5.0 threshold.

recent.statement: The FOMC will raise interest rates at the July 28 meeting
recent.evidence: The July 28 FOMC meeting was still scheduled 8 days out at snapshot and no signal shows any rate decision occurred within the closed window.

recent.statement: A Category 1 tropical cyclone will make landfall in the United States within the next 24 hours.
recent.evidence: S1 shows only a tropical depression (93 km/h) in the Atlantic and S2 a Pacific tropical storm—neither is a Category 1 cyclone making US landfall.

recent.statement: A magnitude 5.0 earthquake will occur in the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: S1 reports a magnitude 5.5M earthquake in Mexico on 7/19/2026 17:45 UTC, within the window and exceeding the magnitude 5.0 threshold.

recent.statement: A Category 1 tropical cyclone will make landfall in the United States within the next 24 hours
recent.evidence: S2 shows FAUSTO-26 active in the East Pacific but provides no evidence of a Category 1 landfall in the United States within the window.

recent.statement: A magnitude 5.0 earthquake will occur in the Scotia Sea.
recent.evidence: The only Scotia Sea signal (S3) is a M5.5 quake on 7/18/2026 19:50 UTC, before the forecast's 7/19–7/20 window, and no signal shows a qualifying quake within the window.
